response.justification: James Smith is a seasoned full-stack web developer with 15+ years of experience, but is fundamentally mismatched for this role on multiple hard requirements. Most critically, he has zero Japanese language proficiency — a non-negotiable listed requirement — speaking only English, Romanian, and basic German. His entire career is anchored in web and cloud technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB), with no meaningful experience in embedded firmware, LAN-switch development, network security protocols (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal), or Linux kernel internals. The few embedded and C/C++ mentions (NI LabWindows, robotic arms at Altom) are peripheral, dated, and do not approach the depth this role demands.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience spanning multiple domains and languages
Demonstrated Linux and open-source fluency, including DevOps, CI/CD, and containerization
Peripheral exposure to embedded and systems-level work (NI LabWindows C/C++, hardware dev at Altom, Sound Position Server in C++)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a stated non-negotiable hard requirement
No experience with embedded networking, LAN-switch firmware, or relevant security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, MAC authentication)
Career is overwhelmingly web/cloud-focused (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB); C/C++ systems programming is minimal and dated

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security protocol experience
